summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a read-only collaborator can complete the OAuth reconnect flow and overwrite `oauthTokenData` on a shared credential even though direct credential updates are forbidden.
expected_security_impact: A read-only collaborator should be unable to patch the credential directly but should still be able to complete the OAuth callback flow and overwrite `oauthTokenData` on the shared credential.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned `n8n@2.10.0` at `8e81f3e31398b04fd6f8cc27cf844980cd382117`, built the reproduction image, executed the focused integration proof, and printed `[evohunt] verified read-only shared credential escalation on credential yje7pOTP9li6KGYc; direct PATCH was blocked but oauthTokenData became attacker-access-token` followed by `[evohunt] SUCCESS: shared OAuth read-to-write escalation reproduced`.
verification_method: The integration proof creates an owner-owned OAuth2 credential, shares it read-only to the attacker, verifies direct `PATCH /credentials/:id` is denied with `403`, starts the OAuth reconnect flow as the attacker, completes the callback through a real local OAuth token endpoint, and then asserts that the shared credential's stored `oauthTokenData.access_token` changed to `attacker-access-token`. `verify.sh` validates those proof fields from the generated proof file.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run printed `PASS test/integration/evohunt.oauth-readwrite-escalation.test.ts` from the focused integration proof.
The verification helper printed `[evohunt] verified read-only shared credential escalation on credential yje7pOTP9li6KGYc; direct PATCH was blocked but oauthTokenData became attacker-access-token`.
The proof file generated during the run recorded `patchStatus=403`, `authStatus=200`, `callbackStatus=200`, `overwrittenAccessToken=attacker-access-token`, and `tokenEndpointHits=1`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
